extraction.one_line_summary: GROVES Kaden won stage 5 from the front after lead-out man SCULLY Tom peeled off, with teammate SMITH Dion sitting up to celebrate second.
tactical_patterns: VINE Jay strategically contested intermediate sprints to build GC time buffer over rival HEWITT Jesse in closeout stage.
SCULLY Tom's extended lead-out allowed GROVES Kaden to launch early and hold power on sloping finish gradient.
Team BikeExchange controlled chase at 35-second gap but delayed catch until 3km, allowing break sprint bonus buffer.
Right barrier positioning proved decisive on sloping finish gradient; riders choosing left barrier were disadvantaged.

rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: positive: Demonstrated ability to sustain long sprint from front on gradient finish without fading.
rider_signals.DAINESE Alberto: negative: Caught completely out of position in finale, finished 50th despite being pre-race sprint favorite.
rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: Tactically mature use of intermediate sprints to secure GC position rather than contesting stage.
rider_signals.CATAFORD Alexander: neutral: Joined early break with GC threats but didn't capitalize on circuit's steep Anderson Street section.
